title: Broiled Salmon & Summer Squash
description: This is one of the simplest ways I cook salmon. A thick piece of salmon roasts under the broiler alongside thin slices of summer squash. Everything becomes soft, buttery, and lightly golden with very little effort.
ingredients: 2 medium summer squash (zucchini, yellow squash, or a mix)
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Place the salmon skin-side down on a baking sheet.
Slice the squash thinly and spread it around the salmon on the pan.
Drizzle the squash generously with olive oil and lightly drizzle the salmon.
Season everything with salt and pepper.
Place the pan on the middle rack of the oven and broil for about 18–20 minutes.
Broil until the salmon is cooked through and lightly golden and the squash is very soft and slightly browned.
notes:
You can use any summer squash you have on hand — zucchini, yellow squash, or a mix.

Use a thick piece of salmon if possible (about 1–1½ inches thick). Thinner fillets may cook faster, so check them earlier.

Cooking time can vary depending on your oven and the thickness of the fish. If the salmon is not quite done, return it to the oven for another minute or two.

Lining the baking sheet with parchment paper makes cleanup much easier.
variation:
A couple of chopped green onions scattered over the pan before broiling add a nice mild bite.

Thin asparagus works well in place of the squash and cooks in about the same amount of time.

optionalSections.title: Optional: Crispy Salmon Skin
optionalSections.intro: After removing the salmon from the pan:
steps: Leave the skin on the baking sheet and place it back under the broiler for another 2–5 minutes, until browned and crispy. It makes a rich, salty little cook's treat.

title: Oven Sweet Potato Fries
description: Thin sweet potato fries tossed with olive oil, salt, and pepper and roasted until soft with lightly browned edges. These are great with a quick garlic aioli or a little hot sauce.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Optional: 1–2 cloves garlic, pressed or finely minced
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Wash the sweet potatoes and trim off the ends. Leave the skins on if you like.
Cut each sweet potato in half, then slice into thin wedges and cut those into fries.
Place the fries on a baking sheet and toss with olive oil, salt, and pepper. If using garlic, add it to the pan.
Spread the fries out so they have space to roast.
Bake for about 20 minutes, until tender with lightly browned edges.
notes:
Cutting the fries thin helps them cook quickly and become tender.

You can cut thicker fries if you prefer—just cook them a little longer until they reach your desired tenderness and browning.

These fries make a great side with the burgers.

optionalSections.title: Optional Garlic Aioli (use Paleo mayonnaise if preferred)
steps: Mix ½ cup mayonnaise with 1 crushed or pressed clove garlic, a squeeze of lemon (about 1–2 teaspoons), a pinch of salt, black pepper, and a little smoked paprika. Serve alongside the fries.

title: Roasted Broccoli with Chili Flakes
description: Broccoli roasted with olive oil, salt, pepper, and a pinch of chili flakes until tender and lightly browned. Simple and full of flavor.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Rinse the broccoli and cut it into florets.
Place the broccoli on a baking sheet and toss with olive oil, salt, pepper, and chili flakes.
Spread the broccoli out so it roasts rather than steams.
Roast for 20–25 minutes, stirring once if you like, until tender and lightly browned.
notes:
Smaller florets cook a little faster and develop more browned edges.

Adjust the chili flakes to your preferred level of heat.

title: Cumin Roasted Cauliflower
description: Cauliflower florets roasted with olive oil, cumin, and smoked paprika until tender and lightly browned. A squeeze of lemon at the end brightens everything up.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
1 teaspoon cumin seeds (or ¾ teaspoon ground cumin)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Rinse the cauliflower and cut it into small florets.
Place the florets on a baking sheet and toss with olive oil, salt, pepper, cumin, and smoked paprika.
Spread the cauliflower out so it roasts rather than steams.
Roast for 20–25 minutes, stirring once or twice, until tender and lightly browned.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon if you like.
notes:
Smaller florets roast faster and get more browned edges.

If using cumin seeds, they add a little extra texture and flavor.

title: Rustic Skillet Beets
description: Thinly sliced beets roast in olive oil with salt, pepper, and oregano until tender and lightly caramelized. If your beets come with greens, they cook right in the pan for an easy rustic side.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
½ teaspoon dried oregano (or 1 tablespoon fresh oregano)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Rinse the beets. Trim off the tops and bottoms and remove any very thick skin near the stem.
Cut the beets into quarters, then slice them thinly.
Place the beets in an oven-safe skillet or baking dish.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, and oregano. Toss lightly to coat.
Roast the beets for about 10–12 minutes.
If using beet greens, loosely chop them and remove any very thick stems. Scatter them over the beets.
Return the pan to the oven and roast another 10–15 minutes. About halfway through, stir everything together so the greens soften in the oil and beet juices.
Continue cooking until the beets are tender and lightly caramelized.
notes:
Slicing the beets thinly helps them cook faster and become tender.

A cast-iron skillet works especially well for caramelizing the edges.

A small drizzle of olive oil over the greens helps them soften as they cook.

title: Easy Curry Beef & Cabbage
description: This easy one-pan dish bakes ground beef under a layer of cabbage with warm curry spices. The beef cooks in a little water, creating a light broth that the cabbage absorbs as it softens. It's simple, cozy, and surprisingly flavorful.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
½ teaspoon curry powder (for the cabbage)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In an oven-safe skillet or pan, add the ground beef and water.
Break up the beef with a spoon or fork and mix it into the water.
Season the beef mixture with curry powder, salt, and pepper.
Spread the chopped cabbage evenly over the top of the beef.
Drizzle the cabbage lightly with olive oil and season with a little more curry powder, salt, and pepper.
Bake for about 20 minutes, until the cabbage is tender and lightly browned on top.
Remove from the oven and stir everything together so the cabbage absorbs the flavorful broth.
notes:
Any oven-safe skillet or baking dish will work. Cast iron works especially well.

The dish will have a light curry broth after baking, which the cabbage absorbs when stirred.

optionalSections.title: Serving Suggestion
steps: Serve as is, or with rice if you want something to soak up the broth.

title: Simple Baked Burgers & Mushrooms
description: This is an easy one-pan meal. The burgers cook on a rack so they brown nicely while mushrooms roast underneath and soak up some of the flavor.
ingredients: A few handfuls button or cremini mushrooms (whatever fits around the burgers on the pan)
½ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Optional spices: pinch garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, or a small pinch of asafoetida (hing)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Divide the ground beef into four patties (about ¼ lb each).
Season both sides of the burgers with salt, pepper, and any optional spices.
Place the burgers on a wire rack set over a baking sheet.
Scatter the mushrooms around or under the rack on the baking sheet.
Drizzle the mushrooms with olive oil and season lightly with salt and pepper.
Bake for about 20 minutes, until the mushrooms are tender and the burgers are cooked to medium-rare.
notes:
Using a rack allows the burgers to brown while the mushrooms cook underneath.

Cooking time may vary slightly depending on burger thickness and your oven.

Medium-rare is ideal here — cook a few minutes longer if you prefer them more well-done.

optionalSections.title: Serving Suggestion
steps: Serve with a fermented pickle and a little mustard for brightness and balance.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Oven Sweet Potato Fries.

title: Crispy Roast Chicken & Potatoes
description: This is my favorite simple way to cook a whole chicken. A blast of high heat crisps the skin, then the oven is lowered so the chicken finishes slowly and stays juicy. Yukon gold potatoes roast alongside the chicken and become soft, buttery, and crispy on the outside.
ingredients: ½ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
1–2 teaspoons dried herbs (such as thyme, rosemary, or herbes de Provence)
Optional: light sprinkle of garlic powder or onion powder
instructions: Preheat the oven to 500°F.
Place the chicken breast-side up on a wire rack set over a baking sheet.
Season the chicken generously with salt, pepper, and herbs.
Rinse the potatoes and place them whole on the rack or around the chicken on the pan.
Roast the chicken at 500°F for 20 minutes to begin crisping the skin.
Lower the oven temperature to 300°F and continue roasting for about 1 hour, until the chicken is cooked through and the juices run clear.
Check the potatoes during cooking. If they are very tender when pierced with a fork, remove them and set aside.
Let the chicken rest for about 10 minutes before carving.
notes:
Cooking time will vary depending on the size of the chicken and your oven.

The chicken is done when the internal temperature reaches 165°F at the thickest part of the thigh.

Using a wire rack allows air to circulate around the chicken so the skin crisps better and the chicken stays out of the drippings.

Yukon Gold potatoes become very soft and buttery inside while their skins crisp in the oven.

To serve, split the potatoes and add butter, salt, and black pepper. I'll sometimes add a spoonful of yogurt or sour cream, and a little hot sauce over everything. Good-quality butter or cultured dairy makes it really nice.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: A simple green salad balances the richness of the chicken and potatoes.
Simple Steamed Zucchini.

title: Simple Pan-Seared Lamb Chops
description: This is my favorite simple way to cook lamb chops. A hot pan, good salt, and a little thyme bring out the rich flavor of the lamb. Browning the edges adds extra depth and texture.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
½ teaspoon dried thyme (or a few fresh thyme leaves)
instructions: Pat the lamb chops dry with a paper towel. Season both sides with salt, pepper, and thyme.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Place the lamb chops in the pan with a little space around each one.
Sear the first side until nicely browned, about 3–4 minutes.
Flip the chops and cook another 2–3 minutes on the second side.
Using tongs, hold the chops on their sides and brown the fat edges for about 30–60 seconds.
Transfer the lamb chops to a plate and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
notes: Use a pan large enough so the lamb chops have space around them. This allows them to brown properly instead of steaming. Lamb chops are best when slightly pink in the center.

optionalSections.title: Optional Pan Toss (Sweet Arugula)
optionalSections.intro: After removing the lamb chops from the pan:
steps: Add 2–3 chopped dates and a small handful of chopped pecans to the warm lamb fat.
Cook for about 1 minute, until fragrant.
Toss in a few handfuls of arugula and stir until it just wilts.
Transfer to a plate and finish with crumbled soft goat cheese.
optionalSections.outro: Serve the lamb chops on top.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Great with Oven Sweet Potato Fries.

title: Five-Spice Beef Meatballs
description: Ground beef mixed with red onion and Chinese five-spice, rolled into large meatballs and quickly browned in a hot skillet. They stay juicy with a slightly pink center and lots of warm spice flavor.
ingredients: ½ medium red onion, finely chopped
instructions: In a bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped red onion, five-spice powder, and salt.
Mix everything together with your hands until evenly combined.
Divide the mixture into 8 portions and roll them into large meatballs.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook the meatballs for about 6–8 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned on the outside and still slightly pink inside.
notes:
Red onion adds a little sweetness that works well with the five-spice.

Green onion can also be used in place of red onion if you prefer.

These have no binder, so handle them gently.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Instant Pot Mashed Potatoes with Parsnip.

optionalSections.title: Optional Spinach
steps: Add a couple handfuls of spinach to the warm pan and let it wilt in the beef fat for a quick side.

title: Instant Pot Mashed Potatoes with Parsnip & Zucchini
description: Creamy mashed potatoes cooked in the Instant Pot with a parsnip and zucchini for extra flavor and softness. Finished with butter and black pepper.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Optional topping: yogurt or sour cream
instructions: Trim any rough or discolored spots from the potatoes. Leave most of the skin on, removing only thick patches if needed.
Cut the potatoes into roughly ½-inch chunks.
Trim the parsnip and cut it into slightly smaller pieces.
Place the potatoes and parsnip in the Instant Pot. Add the water and salt.
Turn on the Instant Pot. Once the water begins heating but before the lid seals, add the chopped zucchini.
Seal the lid and cook on high pressure for 8 minutes.
Quick-release the pressure when finished.
Drain most of the water, leaving a small amount in the pot.
Add the butter and black pepper and mash everything with a fork until creamy.
notes:
Leaving a little of the cooking water helps the potatoes mash smoothly.

Suggested topping: a spoonful of yogurt or sour cream.

title: Instant Pot Beef Bone Broth
description: A simple, rich beef bone broth made in the Instant Pot. Garlic and herbs add depth, making it perfect for drinking on its own or using as a base for soups.
ingredients: 1 large beef soup bone (or 2 smaller bones)
1 whole garlic bulb, or ½ bulb if very fresh
Water (to the Instant Pot fill line)
instructions: Place the beef bone in the Instant Pot.
Add the garlic bulb, salt, and vinegar.
Fill the pot with water up to the fill line.
Seal the lid and cook on High Pressure for 3 hours.
Allow the pressure to release naturally if possible.
Once the lid can be opened, add the rosemary and thyme, crushing them slightly between your fingers as you add them.
Stir, cover loosely, and let the herbs steep for about 20 minutes.
Remove the bone and garlic, then taste and adjust the salt if needed.
notes:
Vinegar helps draw minerals and flavor from the bone.

Crushing the dried herbs between your fingers helps release their aroma.

This broth is great for sipping or as a base for soups and beans.

title: Instant Pot Chicken Bone Broth with Ginger
description: A nourishing chicken bone broth made in the Instant Pot with turmeric and fresh ginger. The result is a flavorful broth that's great for sipping or using as a base for soups.
ingredients: Water (to cover the bones, up to the Instant Pot fill line)
instructions: Place the chicken bones in the Instant Pot.
Add the salt, vinegar, turmeric, and sliced ginger.
Fill the pot with water until the bones are covered, up to the fill line.
Seal the lid and cook on High Pressure for 2 hours.
Allow the pressure to release naturally.
Open the lid and remove the bones and ginger.
Taste and adjust the salt if needed.
notes:
Turmeric and ginger give the broth a warm flavor and a golden color.

This broth is excellent for sipping on its own or using as a base for soups.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Serving
steps: Warm a mug of broth and add a squeeze of lemon and a little extra salt.

title: Smoked Paprika Roasted Turnips
description: Turnips roasted with olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, and oregano until soft and lightly browned. A simple, flavorful side that pairs especially well with lamb.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Wash the turnips and trim the tops and bottoms.
Cut the turnips into quarters, then slice them thinly.
Spread the turnips on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper if you like.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, cumin, and oregano. Toss to coat.
Spread them out so they roast rather than steam.
Roast for about 20 minutes, stirring once if you like, until soft and lightly browned.
notes:
Turnips release some liquid as they cook, so spreading them out on the pan helps them roast instead of steam.

Also good as a base for taco fillings.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Broiled Pork Chops.

title: Broiled Pork Chops
description: Pork chops broiled until nicely browned with crispy edges of fat. A simple method that produces juicy, flavorful chops in minutes.
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Optional: pinch smoked paprika or oregano
instructions: Preheat the broiler.
Pat the pork chops dry with a paper towel.
Rub the chops lightly with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, and any optional spices.
Place the chops on a broiler pan or baking sheet.
Position the pan on the second rack from the top of the oven.
Broil for about 4 minutes per side, until browned and the fat begins to crisp.
notes:
Cooking time may vary depending on the thickness of the chops and your broiler.

Pork chops are done when the internal temperature reaches 145°F. Let them rest for a few minutes before serving.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Smoked Paprika Roasted Turnips.

title: Ginger Tofu with Leeks & Shiitake
description: Tofu sautéed with leeks, shiitake mushrooms, fresh ginger, tamari, and black pepper. A simple one-pan dish with rich, savory flavor.
ingredients: 1 cup fresh shiitake mushrooms, roughly chopped
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced leek and shiitake mushrooms and cook until fragrant and the mushrooms soften.
Cut the tofu into small cubes.
Add the tofu to the pan.
Grate the ginger over the tofu and add the tamari and black pepper.
Stir and cook for several minutes until everything is heated through and the tofu absorbs the flavor.
notes:
Draining the tofu first helps it absorb the tamari.

Serve on top of quinoa or a baked sweet potato.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Sesame Cucumber Salad.

title: Warm Blueberries with Yogurt & Pumpkin Seeds
description: A simple bowl of gently warmed blueberries with creamy yogurt and crunchy pumpkin seeds.
instructions: Place the blueberries in a bowl or mug.
Microwave briefly, about 30–60 seconds, just until slightly warmed.
Spoon the yogurt over the berries.
Add the vanilla stevia if using.
Sprinkle pumpkin seeds on top.
Add fresh blackberries if you like.
notes:
The berries should be just warmed, not fully cooked.

Suggested serving: breakfast or a simple dessert.

title: Apple, Banana & Peanut Butter Yogurt Bowl
description: Fresh fruit with yogurt and crunchy peanut butter. A quick, simple bowl that works for breakfast or a light dessert.
instructions: Place the chopped apple, banana, and strawberries in a bowl.
Spoon the yogurt over the fruit.
Add the peanut butter.
Stir gently.

title: Prunes with Roasted Walnuts
description: Sweet prunes with crunchy roasted walnuts. A simple, naturally sweet dessert or snack.
instructions: Place the prunes in a small bowl.
Add the roasted walnuts on top or alongside.
notes: Roasted pumpkin seeds also work well. A dried fig can be added for variety.

title: Simple Greens with Avocado & Hemp Seeds
description: Fresh greens with creamy avocado, hemp seeds, and a light vinaigrette. Red bell pepper adds a touch of sweetness and color, making this a simple, everyday salad you can build on.
ingredients: 2 cups spring mix or mixed greens
½ ripe avocado, sliced or cubed
¼ cup chopped red bell pepper
1–2 teaspoons vinegar (balsamic, ume plum, or apple cider)
instructions: Place the greens in a bowl.
Add the avocado, hemp seeds, and red bell pepper.
Drizzle with olive oil and vinegar.
Season with salt and pepper.
Toss gently to combine.
notes:
This is a simple base salad. Add anything with color or crunch to make it your own — thinly sliced carrot, shredded purple cabbage, seeds, fresh herbs, or sprouts all work well.

Pairs well with Rustic Skillet Beets or Oven Sweet Potato Fries.

title: Creamy Grated Cabbage Slaw
description: Finely grated cabbage and carrot tossed with mayonnaise, salt, and pepper. A simple slaw with a soft, creamy texture.
ingredients: Optional: thinly sliced red bell pepper
instructions: Grate the cabbage using the larger holes of a box grater.
Grate the carrot using the finer holes.
Place the cabbage and carrot in a bowl.
Add the mayonnaise, salt, and pepper.
Toss until everything is lightly coated.
notes: The finely grated vegetables give this slaw a softer texture than traditional coleslaw.

title: Sesame Cucumber Salad
description: Thinly sliced cucumbers tossed with sesame oil and rice vinegar. A light, refreshing salad with simple savory flavor.
instructions: Trim the ends of the cucumbers.
Peel away about one-third of the skin in strips if you like.
Slice the cucumbers thinly into rounds or half-moons.
Place them in a bowl.
Add the sesame oil, rice vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Toss gently to combine.
Sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds if using.
notes: Good alongside Ginger Tofu with Leeks & Shiitake.

title: Roasted Delicata Squash
description: Thin slices of delicata squash roasted with olive oil, smoked paprika, and garlic salt until tender and lightly browned.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Trim the ends off the squash.
Cut it in half lengthwise and scrape out the seeds.
Slice the squash into thin half-moons.
Place the slices on a baking sheet.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with garlic salt and smoked paprika. Toss to coat.
Spread the squash out so the slices can roast rather than steam.
Roast for 15–20 minutes, stirring once if you like, until tender and lightly browned.
notes: The skin of delicata squash is tender and can be eaten.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Instant Pot Mashed Potatoes with Parsnip.

title: Garlic Herb Pinto Beans
description: Tender pinto beans finished with butter, olive oil, garlic, thyme, and rosemary. A rich, comforting side dish.
ingredients: ½ teaspoon sea salt (plus more to taste)
1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, finely chopped (or 1 teaspoon dried)
Optional: lemon zest or a squeeze of lemon
instructions: Rinse the dried pinto beans, then place them in the Instant Pot and cover with several inches of water.
Cook on High Pressure for 30 minutes.
Drain and rinse the beans.
Return the beans to the Instant Pot, cover with fresh water just to cover, add the salt, and cook on High Pressure for another 30 minutes.
Drain most of the cooking water, leaving the beans moist but not soupy. Transfer the beans to a bowl.
Rinse out the Instant Pot and set it to Sauté. Add the butter and olive oil.
When the butter melts, add the garlic and cook briefly until fragrant, without browning.
Add the thyme and rosemary.
Pour the garlic-herb mixture over the beans and stir lightly to combine.
Season with black pepper and add lemon zest or a squeeze of lemon if you like.
notes: These beans are especially good served on buttered bread or alongside steak.

title: Quick Skillet Greens with Coriander
description: Tender greens quickly cooked in coconut oil with toasted coriander seeds. A simple, flavorful side dish.
ingredients: 1 small container baby cooking greens (such as baby chard or power greens)
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the coconut oil.
Add the coriander seeds and cook briefly until fragrant.
Add a pinch of salt and a couple generous grinds of fresh black pepper.
Add the greens and toss until wilted and tender. Let them brown slightly if you like.
notes: If you don't have coriander seeds, use ¼ teaspoon ground coriander instead — add it with the salt and pepper, just before the greens.

title: Simple Red Lentil Dal
description: A warm, nourishing lentil soup made with cumin, coriander, ginger, and curry powder. Simple, comforting, and full of flavor.
instructions: Turn the Instant Pot to Sauté and add the coconut oil.
Add the cumin and coriander seeds and cook briefly until fragrant.
Add the diced onion and carrots and cook until the onion becomes translucent.
Add the garlic and grated ginger and cook briefly.
Turn off the heat and stir in the curry powder and a generous pinch of salt.
Pour in the broth or water, scraping the bottom of the pot to loosen any browned bits.
Add the rinsed lentils and stir.
Cook on High Pressure for 12 minutes.
Allow the pressure to release naturally, or use a quick release.
Stir in the ghee if using.
Taste and adjust salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Top with chopped cilantro if using.
notes:
This dal is meant to be a slightly thinner soup rather than a thick stew.

A squeeze of fresh lemon at the end brightens the flavor.

You can use homemade chicken broth (see the Chicken Bone Broth recipe) or water.

If you prefer not to use ghee, a little extra coconut oil stirred in at the end makes the soup rich and satisfying.

You can also stir in a handful of chopped baby spinach just before serving and let it wilt in the hot soup.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Quick Skillet Greens with Coriander.

title: Teriyaki Roasted Portobello Mushrooms
description: Portobello mushrooms roasted with a simple teriyaki-style marinade. Savory, slightly sweet, and full of flavor.
ingredients: 2–4 large portobello mushrooms, trimmed and cleaned
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Place the mushrooms on a baking sheet.
In a small bowl, mix the olive oil, tamari, maple syrup, ginger, and garlic.
Spoon or brush the mixture over the mushrooms.
Roast for about 20 minutes, until tender.
Remove from the oven and slice the mushrooms.
Toss the sliced mushrooms in the pan juices.
Return to the oven for another 1–2 minutes if needed.
notes:
The mushrooms will absorb the sauce as they cook, so they should be well coated and juicy.

Adjust sweetness and salt to taste.

Good with rice, greens, or simple sides.

optionalSections.title: Suggested Side
steps: Sesame Cucumber Salad.
Simple Steamed Zucchini.

title: Apricot Cardamom Chia Pudding
description: A creamy chia pudding with coconut milk and freshly ground cardamom. Lightly sweet, rich, and aromatic, with fresh apricots and pistachios.
ingredients: ¼ cup agave, plus more to taste
½ teaspoon cardamom (freshly ground if possible)
instructions: In a bowl, combine the chia seeds and water. Let sit for about 20 minutes to soften.
Add the coconut milk, agave, and cardamom.
Stir well and let sit for a few minutes, then stir again to prevent clumping.
Taste and adjust sweetness if needed before chilling.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1–2 hours, or overnight, until thickened.
Stir before serving, then lightly fold in the apricots and pistachios.
notes:
For the best flavor, grind whole cardamom seeds using a mortar and pestle. Ground cardamom also works.

You can use your preferred sweetener, adjusting the amount to taste.

Ripe fruit makes a real difference here. Let apricots soften on the counter until they're sweet and fragrant. The same goes for blackberries — fully ripe ones are noticeably better.

Blackberries with pistachios are another favorite — other fruits and nuts work well too.

title: Simple Steamed Zucchini
description: A quick, tender way to cook zucchini with just a few ingredients. Light, simple, and easy to pair with almost anything.
instructions: Add about 1 inch of water to a pan and bring to a boil.
Chop the zucchini into large pieces (about ½ inch or slightly larger).
Add the zucchini to the pan, cover, and reduce heat to low once boiling.
Simmer for about 7 minutes, until tender.
Pour off most of the cooking water.
Add a little olive oil or butter, salt, and black pepper. Toss gently.
notes:
This is a soft, simple preparation — cook less if you prefer more bite.

Good alongside roasted meats or simple plates.
